title: Annotate Gene Function with Uproc
author: James E Thornton Jr
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.kt8cwrw

steps.instruction: Log into the HPC. $ ssh hpc$ ocelote $ ssh hpc $ ocelote
Move into your anvio-genes directory. $ cd /rsgrps/bh_class/username/anvio-genes
Move into the function directory. $ cd function

Create uproc_function.sh to run the functional analysis. Your gene calls should be located in /rsgrps/bh_class/username/anvio-genes. It maybe called nucleotides.fna or nucleotides.faa. Make sure the FASTA variable uses the correct name. #!/bin/bash#PBS -W group_list=bh_class#PBS -q windfall#PBS -l select=1:ncpus=12:mem=23gb#PBS -l pvmem=22gb#PBS -l walltime=24:00:00#PBS -l cput=24:00:00#PBS -M netid@email.arizona.edu#PBS -m bea#--------------EDIT THESE---------------FASTA= Make sure to replace netid AND the #!/bin/bash #PBS -W group_list=bh_class #PBS -q windfall #PBS -l select=1:ncpus=12:mem=23gb #PBS -l pvmem=22gb #PBS -l walltime=24:00:00 #PBS -l cput=24:00:00 #PBS -M netid@email.arizona.edu #PBS -m bea #--------------EDIT THESE--------------- FASTA="/rsgrps/bh_class/username/anvio-genes/nucleotides.fna" OUT_DIR="/rsgrps/bh_class/username/anvio-genes/function" OUTPUT="$OUT_DIR/uproc-out" #--------------------------------------- export UPROC="/rsgrps/bh_class/bin/uproc-dna" export DATA="/rsgrps/bh_class/data/uproc" export UPROC_MODEL="$DATA/model" export UPROC_OUT_DIR="$OUT_DIR/uproc-out" export PFAM="$DATA/pfam27ready" export KEGG="$DATA/keggready" $UPROC --preds --short --threads 12 --output $OUTPUT.pfam $PFAM $UPROC_MODEL $FASTA $UPROC --preds --short --threads 12 --output $OUTPUT.kegg $KEGG $UPROC_MODEL $FASTA Make sure to replace netid AND the "EDIT THESE" section
Make a standard out and standard error directory. $ mkdir std-err std-out
Run the uproc_function.sh script. $ qsub -e std-err -o std-out uproc_function.sh

Create a perl script called format-anvio.pl to convert the functional data into anvio format. #! /usr/bin/env perluse strict;if (@ARGV != 4) { die $out") || die "Cannot open out
"; my %id_to_desc; while () { chomp $_; my ($id, $desc) = split (/	/, $_); $id_to_desc{$id} = $desc; } print OUT "gene_callers_id	source	accession	function	e_value
"; while () { chomp $_; my @fields = split (/,/, $_); my $gene = $fields[1]; $gene =~ s/\|.*//; my $id = $fields[6]; my $score = $fields[7]; my $desc = "NONE"; if (exists $id_to_desc{$id}) { $desc = $id_to_desc{$id}; } print OUT "$gene	$source	$id	$desc	$score
"; }" description="" os_version="" os_name="" guid="6C3E63EF8BD949699B4987D94603239E" > #! /usr/bin/env perl use strict; if (@ARGV != 4) { die "Usage: format-anvio.pl uproc-file function-to-desc out source
"; } my $uproc = shift @ARGV; my $function = shift @ARGV; my $out = shift @ARGV; my $source = shift @ARGV; open (F, $function) || die "I need a kegg or pfam desc file
"; open (U, $uproc) || die "I need the uproc file
"; open (OUT, ">$out") || die "Cannot open out
"; my %id_to_desc; while () { chomp $_; my ($id, $desc) = split (/	/, $_); $id_to_desc{$id} = $desc; } print OUT "gene_callers_id	source	accession	function	e_value
"; while () { chomp $_; my @fields = split (/,/, $_); my $gene = $fields[1]; $gene =~ s/\|.*//; my $id = $fields[6]; my $score = $fields[7]; my $desc = "NONE"; if (exists $id_to_desc{$id}) { $desc = $id_to_desc{$id}; } print OUT "$gene	$source	$id	$desc	$score
"; }

Run format-anvio.pl to convert the functional data into anvio format. chmod 755 format-anvio.pl./format-anvio.pl uproc-out.kegg /rsgrps/bh_class/kegg_to_desc uproc-kegg-anvio kegg./format-anvio.pl uproc-out.pfam /rsgrps/bh_class/pfam_to_domain uproc-pfam-anvio pfamcat uproc-kegg-anvio > input_matrix.txtegrep -v > input_matrix.txt" description="" os_version="" os_name="" guid="0E5A90A489E54A29865BA84C39D0AA26" > chmod 755 format-anvio.pl ./format-anvio.pl uproc-out.kegg /rsgrps/bh_class/kegg_to_desc uproc-kegg-anvio kegg ./format-anvio.pl uproc-out.pfam /rsgrps/bh_class/pfam_to_domain uproc-pfam-anvio pfam cat uproc-kegg-anvio > input_matrix.txt egrep -v "gene_callers_id" uproc-pfam-anvio >> input_matrix.txt
Check the status of your job. Continue to the next step upon sucessful job completion. $ qstat -u username

Download the functional data to your computer. Go to the directory where you are storing your Anvi'o data (contigs.db). scp netid@sftp.hpc.arizona.edu:/rsgrps/bh_class/username/anvio-genes/function/input_matrix.txt . This step is done on a local terminal (not the HPC).

Download the taxonomy data to your computer. Go to the directory where you are storing your Anvi'o data (contigs.db). scp netid@sftp.hpc.arizona.edu:/rsgrps/bh_class/username/anvio-genes/taxonomy/*centrifuge_report.tsv .scp netid@sftp.hpc.arizona.edu:/rsgrps/bh_class/username/anvio-genes/taxonomy/*centrifuge_hits.tsv . scp netid@sftp.hpc.arizona.edu:/rsgrps/bh_class/username/anvio-genes/taxonomy/*centrifuge_report.tsv . scp netid@sftp.hpc.arizona.edu:/rsgrps/bh_class/username/anvio-genes/taxonomy/*centrifuge_hits.tsv .

From the Anvi'o terminal, type the following command to upload the taxonomic data for the gene calls. anvi-import-taxonomy -c contigs.db -i centrifuge_report.tsv centrifuge_hits.tsv -p centrifuge You should be in the directory that contains both your contig database and the centrifuge files.
Open Anvio. For those using the Docker image only execute the following command: docker run --rm -v ~/path/to/files:/my_data -p 8080:8080 -it meren/anvio:latest
From the Anvi'o terminal, type the following command to upload the functional data for the gene calls. anvi-import-functions -c contigs.db -i input_matrix.txt

Please document these steps in the methods section of your report. Note what programs you used for each step and what the parameters were. How many genes did Anvi'o find? Is this different from your analyses? Why? Anvi'o uses the "-p meta" parameter for metagenomics datasets, which is stricter than what you first ran. How many of your genes from Anvio had a match to a known bacteria? How many matched known proteins for kegg or pfam?
Move into your function directory. $ cd /rsgrps/bh_class/username/anvio-genes/function
